title: Extract rotation scripts into versioned ConfigMaps
description:
# GitHub Issue #191: Extract embedded rotation scripts into versioned, testable ConfigMaps

**Category**: refactoring | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Replace the inline Go string constants fernetRotateScript and credentialRotateScript (in reconcile_fernet.go and reconcile_credential.go) with externally managed script files embedded via go:embed. Mount them as ConfigMaps in the rotation CronJob pods. This enables linting (shellcheck, pylint), unit testing of script logic, and independent versioning of the rotation logic.

**Motivation**: The current ~40-line scripts mixing shell, Python, and Kubernetes API calls are embedded as raw Go constants with no linting, type checking, or unit testing. A bug in these scripts silently breaks key rotation — one of the most security-critical operations in the system. Extracting them makes them independently testable and auditable.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Scripts extracted to standalone files under a scripts/ or embed/ directory
- [ ] Scripts embedded via go:embed and mounted as ConfigMap volumes
- [ ] shellcheck passes on shell portions of the scripts
- [ ] Script logic has at least basic unit/integration tests
- [ ] Rotation CronJobs function identically to before extraction

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/191

stories.title: Developer can lint and test rotation scripts independently
stories.want: Fernet and credential rotation scripts extracted to standalone .sh files under a scripts/ directory
stories.so_that: I can run shellcheck, write unit tests, and audit script logic without parsing Go string constants
criteria: fernet_rotate.sh and credential_rotate.sh exist as standalone files under operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/
Both scripts have #!/bin/sh shebangs and pass shellcheck without warnings
Scripts are embedded via go:embed into Go string variables at compile time
Embedded content matches the original inline constants byte-for-byte (excluding added shebang)

stories.title: Rotation CronJobs execute scripts from mounted ConfigMaps
stories.want: rotation scripts delivered as immutable ConfigMaps mounted into CronJob pods
stories.so_that: script content is versioned via content-hash naming and CronJobs update automatically when scripts change
criteria: Each rotation reconciler creates an immutable ConfigMap containing its script using CreateImmutableConfigMap
ConfigMap names follow the {name}-{type}-rotate-script-{hash} pattern
CronJob pods mount the script ConfigMap at /scripts/ with DefaultMode 0555 (executable)
CronJob container command changed from inline 'sh -c <script>' to '/scripts/{script}.sh'

stories.title: Operator user sees identical rotation behavior after extraction
stories.want: Fernet and credential rotation CronJobs to function identically after script extraction
stories.so_that: key rotation remains reliable and I don't need to change my deployment or monitoring
criteria: Fernet rotation CronJob still runs keystone-manage fernet_rotate and patches the Secret via K8s API
Credential rotation CronJob still runs credential_rotate, credential_migrate, and patches the Secret
All existing CronJob properties unchanged: schedule, image, env vars, RBAC, security context, restart policy
Existing E2E Chainsaw rotation tests pass without modification

stories.title: Security auditor can independently verify rotation logic
stories.want: rotation scripts in standalone files with clear boundaries between shell and Python logic
stories.so_that: I can audit the security-critical key rotation code without navigating Go source files
criteria: Script files are self-contained and readable without Go context
Shell and Python portions are clearly delineated via heredoc boundaries
Scripts use only Python standard library (no additional image dependencies)
Service account token authentication to K8s API is visible and auditable in the script

stories.title: CI validates shell script quality via shellcheck
stories.want: a Makefile target that runs shellcheck on all rotation scripts
stories.so_that: shell script bugs are caught before merge, preventing silent rotation failures
criteria: make shellcheck target exists and runs shellcheck on operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/*.sh
Both fernet_rotate.sh and credential_rotate.sh pass shellcheck without warnings
shellcheck correctly handles Python heredoc sections without false positives
Target can be integrated into CI pipeline alongside existing lint target

requirements.description: Rotation scripts SHALL be extracted to standalone .sh files with POSIX shebangs under operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/
requirements.rationale: Standalone files enable shellcheck linting, independent testing, and auditing without parsing Go source

scenarios.name: Fernet script file exists with correct content
scenarios.when: the scripts/ directory is inspected
scenarios.then: fernet_rotate.sh exists with #!/bin/sh shebang and contains the fernet_rotate keystone-manage command
and_then: script content matches the original fernetRotateScript constant (plus shebang)

scenarios.name: Credential script file exists with correct content
scenarios.when: the scripts/ directory is inspected
scenarios.then: credential_rotate.sh exists with #!/bin/sh shebang and contains credential_rotate AND credential_migrate commands
and_then: script content matches the original credentialRotateScript constant (plus shebang)

scenarios.name: Scripts use only POSIX shell features
scenarios.when: scripts are analyzed for shell compatibility
scenarios.then: only POSIX-compliant constructs are used (set -e, command execution, heredoc)
and_then: #!/bin/sh shebang is appropriate and no bash-specific features are present

requirements.description: Scripts SHALL be embedded into Go package-level string variables via //go:embed directives
requirements.rationale: go:embed includes file content at compile time, ensuring scripts are part of the operator binary without runtime file access

scenarios.name: Fernet script is embedded via go:embed
scenarios.when: reconcile_fernet.go is compiled
scenarios.then: the fernetRotateScript variable contains the content of scripts/fernet_rotate.sh
and_then: the variable is accessible as a string at runtime

scenarios.name: Credential script is embedded via go:embed
scenarios.when: reconcile_credential.go is compiled
scenarios.then: the credentialRotateScript variable contains the content of scripts/credential_rotate.sh
and_then: the //nolint:gosec directive is removed since go:embed variables don't trigger G101

scenarios.name: Blank embed import is present
scenarios.when: reconcile_fernet.go and reconcile_credential.go are inspected
scenarios.then: both files import _ "embed" to enable go:embed functionality
and_then: go vet passes without 'go:embed requires import of embed' errors

requirements.description: Script content SHALL be delivered as immutable ConfigMaps using the existing CreateImmutableConfigMap pattern with content-hash naming
requirements.rationale: Reusing the established immutable ConfigMap pattern ensures script versioning, automatic CronJob updates on content change, and ownership-based garbage collection

scenarios.name: Fernet script ConfigMap created during reconciliation
scenarios.when: reconcileFernetKeys runs with the fernet keys Secret already existing
scenarios.then: an immutable ConfigMap is created with name matching {keystoneName}-fernet-rotate-script-{hash}
and_then: ConfigMap data has key 'fernet_rotate.sh' with the embedded script content
ConfigMap has owner reference to the Keystone CR

scenarios.name: Credential script ConfigMap created during reconciliation
scenarios.when: reconcileCredentialKeys runs with the credential keys Secret already existing
scenarios.then: an immutable ConfigMap is created with name matching {keystoneName}-credential-rotate-script-{hash}
and_then: ConfigMap data has key 'credential_rotate.sh' with the embedded script content

scenarios.name: Script ConfigMap creation failure returns error
scenarios.when: CreateImmutableConfigMap fails for the script ConfigMap
scenarios.then: reconcileFernetKeys/reconcileCredentialKeys returns a wrapped error
and_then: the CronJob is NOT created or updated
the condition is not set to True

requirements.description: Rotation CronJobs SHALL mount script ConfigMaps as executable volumes at /scripts/ and execute scripts from the mount path
requirements.rationale: Mounting scripts as volumes enables the CronJob to execute the script from a file path instead of inline shell, making the pod spec cleaner and the script independently observable

scenarios.name: Fernet CronJob has script volume with correct ConfigMap reference
scenarios.when: the fernet rotation CronJob spec is inspected
scenarios.then: a volume named 'scripts' exists sourced from the script ConfigMap with DefaultMode 0555
and_then: the main container has a volume mount for 'scripts' at /scripts/ (read-only)

scenarios.name: Credential CronJob has script volume with correct ConfigMap reference
scenarios.when: the credential rotation CronJob spec is inspected
scenarios.then: a volume named 'scripts' exists sourced from the script ConfigMap with DefaultMode 0555
and_then: the main container has a volume mount for 'scripts' at /scripts/ (read-only)

scenarios.name: CronJob command executes from mount path
scenarios.when: the main container command is inspected
scenarios.then: the command is ['/scripts/fernet_rotate.sh'] or ['/scripts/credential_rotate.sh'] respectively
and_then: the inline 'sh -c <script>' pattern is no longer used

requirements.description: Rotation CronJobs SHALL produce identical runtime behavior after script extraction — same commands, same API calls, same error handling
requirements.rationale: Key rotation is the most security-critical operation; any behavioral change risks silent data loss or credential inaccessibility

scenarios.name: Fernet rotation behavior unchanged
scenarios.when: the fernet rotation CronJob executes
scenarios.then: it runs keystone-manage fernet_rotate, reads rotated keys, base64-encodes them, and PATCHes the Secret via K8s API
and_then: all env vars (SECRET_NAME, SECRET_NAMESPACE, OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys) are unchanged
all volume mounts (fernet-keys, credential-keys, config) are still present

scenarios.name: Credential rotation behavior unchanged
scenarios.when: the credential rotation CronJob executes
scenarios.then: it runs credential_rotate, credential_migrate, reads keys, base64-encodes them, and PATCHes the Secret
and_then: all env vars (SECRET_NAME, SECRET_NAMESPACE, OS_credential__max_active_keys) are unchanged

scenarios.name: Init container unchanged
scenarios.when: the CronJob pod starts
scenarios.then: the copy-keys init container still copies keys from the read-only Secret source to the writable emptyDir
and_then: init container image, command, volume mounts, and security context are unchanged

requirements.description: shellcheck SHALL pass on all extracted rotation scripts without warnings
requirements.rationale: Shell linting catches common bugs (unquoted variables, incorrect error handling) that are invisible in Go string constants

scenarios.name: shellcheck passes on fernet_rotate.sh
scenarios.when: shellcheck is run on operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/fernet_rotate.sh
scenarios.then: shellcheck exits with status 0 and produces no warnings
and_then: Python heredoc section is correctly skipped by shellcheck

scenarios.name: shellcheck passes on credential_rotate.sh
scenarios.when: shellcheck is run on operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/credential_rotate.sh
scenarios.then: shellcheck exits with status 0 and produces no warnings

scenarios.name: Makefile target runs shellcheck
scenarios.when: make shellcheck is executed
scenarios.then: shellcheck is run on all .sh files under operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/
and_then: non-zero exit code if any script has warnings

requirements.description: Embedded script content SHALL be verified by Go unit tests to catch embedding errors and content drift
requirements.rationale: A broken go:embed directive or corrupted script file silently produces an empty variable, which would cause CronJob pods to fail at runtime

scenarios.name: Fernet script content is non-empty and valid
scenarios.when: the fernetRotateScript variable is inspected in a unit test
scenarios.then: it is non-empty, starts with #!/bin/sh, and contains 'fernet_rotate'
and_then: it contains the Python heredoc with K8s API PATCH logic

scenarios.name: Credential script content is non-empty and valid
scenarios.when: the credentialRotateScript variable is inspected in a unit test
scenarios.then: it is non-empty, starts with #!/bin/sh, and contains both 'credential_rotate' and 'credential_migrate'
and_then: it contains the Python heredoc with K8s API PATCH logic

scenarios.name: Scripts contain error handling
scenarios.when: the embedded scripts are inspected
scenarios.then: both scripts contain 'set -e' for shell error propagation
and_then: both scripts contain Python error handling for HTTP response status >= 300

requirements.description: Script ConfigMaps SHOULD follow the {keystoneName}-{type}-rotate-script-{hash} naming convention consistent with existing ConfigMap patterns
requirements.rationale: Consistent naming with the existing config ConfigMap pattern ({keystoneName}-config-{hash}) makes resource discovery and debugging predictable

scenarios.name: Fernet script ConfigMap name follows convention
scenarios.when: the fernet script ConfigMap is created
scenarios.then: its name matches the pattern {keystoneName}-fernet-rotate-script-{8-char-hex-hash}
and_then: the hash suffix changes when script content changes

scenarios.name: Credential script ConfigMap name follows convention
scenarios.when: the credential script ConfigMap is created
scenarios.then: its name matches the pattern {keystoneName}-credential-rotate-script-{8-char-hex-hash}
and_then: the hash suffix is deterministic for the same script content

scenarios.name: Different script content produces different ConfigMap names
scenarios.when: the operator is upgraded with modified script content
scenarios.then: CreateImmutableConfigMap produces a new name with a different hash suffix
and_then: EnsureCronJob updates the CronJob to reference the new ConfigMap name

tasks.title: Create fernet_rotate.sh and credential_rotate.sh script files under scripts/ directory (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/ directory. Extract the fernetRotateScript constant body from reconcile_fernet.go:35-56 into scripts/fernet_rotate.sh, prepending #!/bin/sh shebang. Extract credentialRotateScript from reconcile_credential.go:37-59 into scripts/credential_rotate.sh, prepending #!/bin/sh shebang. Ensure both files end with a newline. Verify with shellcheck.

tasks.title: Replace const declarations with go:embed directives in reconcile_fernet.go and reconcile_credential.go (REQ-002)
tasks.description: In reconcile_fernet.go: add '_ "embed"' import, replace 'const fernetRotateScript = `...`' with '//go:embed scripts/fernet_rotate.sh' + 'var fernetRotateScript string'. In reconcile_credential.go: same pattern for credentialRotateScript, also remove the //nolint:gosec G101 comment (no longer needed since go:embed vars don't trigger G101). Update comments above the variables to reference the file paths. Verify: go build ./operators/keystone/... succeeds, go vet passes.

tasks.title: Add Go unit tests verifying embedded script content and structure (REQ-007)
tasks.description: In reconcile_fernet_test.go add TestFernetRotateScript_EmbeddedContent: verify fernetRotateScript is non-empty, starts with '#!/bin/sh', contains 'set -e', contains 'fernet_rotate', contains 'python3', contains 'Secret update failed'. In reconcile_credential_test.go add TestCredentialRotateScript_EmbeddedContent: same checks plus verify it contains both 'credential_rotate' and 'credential_migrate'. These tests guard against broken go:embed directives producing empty strings.

tasks.title: Update reconcileFernetKeys to create script ConfigMap and modify fernetRotationCronJob builder (REQ-003, REQ-004)
tasks.description: In reconcile_fernet.go: (1) Add config import for CreateImmutableConfigMap and ptr import for DefaultMode. (2) In reconcileFernetKeys, after RBAC setup and before CronJob creation, call config.CreateImmutableConfigMap to create a ConfigMap named '{name}-fernet-rotate-script' containing {'fernet_rotate.sh': fernetRotateScript}. Pass the returned scriptConfigMapName to fernetRotationCronJob. (3) Update fernetRotationCronJob signature to accept scriptConfigMapName string parameter. (4) Add 'scripts' volume sourced from scriptConfigMapName ConfigMap with DefaultMode ptr.To(int32(0555)). (5) Add VolumeMount {Name: 'scripts', MountPath: '/scripts', ReadOnly: true} to main container. (6) Change Command from []string{'sh', '-c', fernetRotateScript} to []string{'/scripts/fernet_rotate.sh'}.

tasks.title: Update fernet rotation tests for new CronJob structure (REQ-004, REQ-005)
tasks.description: In reconcile_fernet_test.go: (1) Update TestReconcileFernetKeys_CronJobSpec: change Command assertion to []string{'/scripts/fernet_rotate.sh'}, update volume count from 4 to 5, update volume mount count from 3 to 4, add assertions for 'scripts' volume (ConfigMap source with name prefix 'test-keystone-fernet-rotate-script-', DefaultMode 0555), add assertion for '/scripts' volume mount (read-only). (2) Update TestReconcileFernetKeys_SecretAlreadyExists to verify script ConfigMap is created. (3) Verify all env vars, other volumes, init container, security context, labels assertions are unchanged. (4) Run tests: go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/ -run TestReconcileFernet.

tasks.title: Update reconcileCredentialKeys to create script ConfigMap and modify credentialRotationCronJob builder (REQ-003, REQ-004)
tasks.description: In reconcile_credential.go: mirror the changes from task 2.1. (1) Add config and ptr imports. (2) In reconcileCredentialKeys, create ConfigMap named '{name}-credential-rotate-script' containing {'credential_rotate.sh': credentialRotateScript}. (3) Update credentialRotationCronJob signature to accept scriptConfigMapName. (4) Add 'scripts' volume with DefaultMode 0555. (5) Add '/scripts' volume mount to main container. (6) Change Command to []string{'/scripts/credential_rotate.sh'}.

tasks.title: Update credential rotation tests for new CronJob structure (REQ-004, REQ-005)
tasks.description: In reconcile_credential_test.go: mirror the changes from task 2.2. (1) Update TestReconcileCredentialKeys_CronJobSpec: change Command assertion, update volume/mount counts, add script volume assertions with 'test-keystone-credential-rotate-script-' prefix. (2) Update TestReconcileCredentialKeys_SecretAlreadyExists to verify script ConfigMap creation. (3) Verify credential_rotate and credential_migrate command verification is preserved (script content test, not CronJob command). (4) Run tests: go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/ -run TestReconcileCredential.

tasks.title: Add Makefile shellcheck target for rotation scripts (REQ-006)
tasks.description: In Makefile: add a 'shellcheck' target under a new 'Script Linting' section. Target runs: shellcheck operators/keystone/internal/controller/scripts/*.sh. Add .PHONY declaration. Add comment with CC-0073 reference. Verify: make shellcheck passes. The target should fail with non-zero exit if any script has warnings.

tasks.title: Reference documentation for rotation script ConfigMap pattern (CC-0073)
tasks.description: Create docs/reference/backend/rotation-scripts.md documenting: (1) the go:embed pattern for rotation scripts, (2) the ConfigMap mounting approach with content-hash naming, (3) how to modify rotation scripts (edit .sh file, rebuild operator, ConfigMap auto-updates), (4) how to run shellcheck locally. Keep concise (<80 lines). Reference CC-0073 and existing CreateImmutableConfigMap pattern.

review_criteria: Both scripts pass shellcheck without warnings (make shellcheck exits 0)
go vet and golangci-lint pass for all modified files (no new lint errors introduced)
All existing unit tests pass — only expected assertion values change (Command, volume/mount counts, new volume)
CronJob spec diff shows ONLY: added 'scripts' volume+mount, changed Command — all other fields (env, image, schedule, RBAC, security context) unchanged
CreateImmutableConfigMap from internal/common/config is used for script ConfigMaps (not raw corev1.ConfigMap creation)
restrictedSecurityContext() still applied to all init and main containers (no security regression)
Script file content is byte-identical to original Go constant bodies (plus #!/bin/sh shebang prepended)
Reference documentation exists in docs/reference/backend/rotation-scripts.md explaining the go:embed + ConfigMap pattern
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

This is a pure refactoring that extracts inline Go string constants into standalone script files, embeds them via go:embed, and delivers them as ConfigMap volumes. No behavioral change to rotation logic.

## Data Flow

```
scripts/fernet_rotate.sh (on disk)
  -> //go:embed (compile time) -> fernetRotateScript (Go string var)
  -> CreateImmutableConfigMap (reconcile time) -> ConfigMap with content-hash name
  -> CronJob volume mount -> /scripts/fernet_rotate.sh (pod runtime)
  -> Container executes /scripts/fernet_rotate.sh
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **go:embed per file**: Each reconcile_*.go file embeds its own script via `//go:embed scripts/*.sh` + `var ... string`. This keeps the embedding colocated with usage.

2. **Immutable ConfigMaps with content-hash**: Reuses existing `config.CreateImmutableConfigMap()` (internal/common/config/config.go:130) which handles hash-suffixed naming, ownership, and AlreadyExists verification. When script content changes (operator upgrade), a new ConfigMap name is generated, the CronJob spec changes, and `job.EnsureCronJob` updates it.

3. **DefaultMode 0555**: Script ConfigMap volumes use `DefaultMode: ptr.To(int32(0555))` to make scripts executable. This allows `Command: []string{"/scripts/fernet_rotate.sh"}` without explicit shell invocation.

4. **One ConfigMap per script**: Each reconciler creates its own script ConfigMap. This keeps resource ownership clean and avoids coupling fernet and credential rotation versioning.

5. **Shebang addition**: Scripts get `#!/bin/sh` shebangs (not in original constants) for proper shellcheck support and standalone execution.

## Patterns to Follow

- ConfigMap creation: reconcile_config.go:168-184 (CreateImmutableConfigMap call)
- Volume mounting: reconcile_fernet.go:307-316 (ConfigMap volume pattern)
- CronJob management: job.EnsureCronJob (job.go:119) handles create/update
- Test pattern: Gomega + fake client (reconcile_fernet_test.go)
- ptr.To() usage: reconcile_deployment.go uses k8s.io/utils/ptr

## Potential Pitfalls

1. **go:embed path resolution**: Paths are relative to the source file's package directory (operators/keystone/internal/controller/). The scripts/ subdirectory must be at that level.

2. **CronJob spec hash change**: Adding the scripts volume changes the CronJob spec. EnsureCronJob will update existing CronJobs on the next reconcile after operator upgrade. This is expected and correct.

3. **nolint removal**: credentialRotateScript had `//nolint:gosec // G101` because the const name contained 'credential'. With go:embed, the variable declaration has no string literal, so G101 doesn't trigger. Verify with `golangci-lint run`.

4. **Empty embed variable**: If the go:embed directive path is wrong, the variable silently stays empty string. Task 1.3 adds explicit tests to catch this.

## Deferred Items

- **Script refactoring to share common Python K8s API code**: Both scripts have identical Python PATCH logic. Extracting a shared update_secret.py would reduce duplication but changes script logic (out of scope for extraction-only refactoring). Revisit in a future feature.
- **pylint/flake8 on Python portions**: Python code is embedded in shell heredocs, making standalone Python linting complex. Would require extracting Python to separate files — a larger refactoring. Revisit if Python logic grows.
- **Integration test with mock K8s API**: Testing the actual script execution against a mock API server would provide end-to-end script verification. Requires test infrastructure setup beyond current scope. Existing Chainsaw E2E tests cover this path.
